description:
## Refactor

`src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr:450-457`:

```crystal
def self.force_stuck!(seconds : Int32 = 600) : Nil
  now_ms = Time.utc.to_unix_ms
  @@last_refresh_start.set(now_ms - (seconds * 1000))
  @@last_refresh_complete.set(0)
  Log.for("quickheadlines.watchdog").info { "RefreshHealthMonitor: forced stuck state for testing (#{seconds}s)" }
end
```

This is a **test helper** — its only purpose is to simulate the "stuck refresh" state so other code (e.g., the supervisor's `check_stuck_recovery`, the watchdog logic) can be exercised under test. But it lives on the production class.

The log line even says `"forced stuck state for testing"`.

## Why it matters

- Production binaries ship with a test helper that mutates shared state in unsafe ways (no mutex, no rate limit, no audit trail of who called it).
- The method is public, so any caller (not just tests) could invoke it accidentally.
- Future readers will wonder "is this used in production?" — the log message answers that, but the API does not enforce it.

## Fix

Move the helper to one of:

- `spec/support/refresh_health_monitor_helpers.cr` — a test-only module that requires the spec environment.
- A `RefreshHealthMonitor.test_force_stuck!` method guarded by `if QuickHeadlines.env == "test"` or similar.
- A separate `RefreshHealthMonitor::TestHooks` module that is only `require`d in spec files.

**Recommended**: extract to a test-support module so production binaries have zero knowledge of the test helper.

## Acceptance

- `RefreshHealthMonitor.force_stuck!` is removed from the production class.
- The same functionality is available via a test-support module under `spec/support/` (or equivalent) and is required only by the spec files that use it.
- All existing specs that exercise the stuck-recovery path continue to pass.
- A `grep -rn "force_stuck" src/` returns 0 results (only spec files reference it).
- `just nix-build` passes; existing Crystal specs pass.
title: Refactor: move RefreshHealthMonitor.force_stuck! out of production class
